task: Classify inbound visitor messages for the Concierge router
dataset_sources: Bitext customer support dataset mapped to faq/support/sales_or_leads/human_request
SMS Spam Collection mapped to spam
CLINC OOS plus mapped to other
Hand-labeled realistic concierge examples added for demo-style generalization
data_cleaning: Removed exact duplicate text leakage across train/validation/test
Cleaned template artifacts like {{person name}}, IDs, and long numbers
Augmented training with realistic hand-labeled examples
chosen_model_reason: Chosen for production because it has strong held-out test performance, better realistic-message behavior after augmentation, very low latency, simple joblib serving, and no torch/transformers in the container. The LLM baseline performed very well but is not used for every message because it adds API cost and latency.

final_comparison.notes: Chosen: fast, cheap, local, joblib, no torch
Good, but lower score and more complex
Best manual result, but API cost and latency

llm_baseline.note: LLM zero-shot baseline evaluated on manual realistic examples. Full held-out test was not run because API evaluation has cost and latency.

limitations: Public datasets may contain dataset-specific writing style.
Manual realistic sanity tests are reported separately from held-out public dataset scores.
Classifier is used only as a router; uncertain messages go to the agent.
